title: pedicularis roylei subsp. shawii
intro: Pedicularis roylei subsp. shawii, also known as the Himalayan lousewort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants, typically rhododendrons and grasses. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and it has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Pedicularis roylei subsp. shawii is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support immune function and overall health.
